address: Pak Nam Prasae, Klaeng District, Rayong, Thailand
opening_hours: Public nature-site timing can vary; late-afternoon visits work best when weather and light are cooperative.

description.en: A mangrove-boardwalk stop on the Prasae side that works best in late light and as part of a broader wetland day.
description.nl: Een mangrove-boardwalkstop aan de Prasae-kant die het best werkt in laat licht en als onderdeel van een bredere wetlanddag.

metaDescription.en: Why Thung Prong Thong is one of Rayong's best mangrove stops and how to use it as part of the Prasae-side route.
metaDescription.nl: Waarom Thung Prong Thong een van Rayongs beste mangrovestops is en hoe je het gebruikt in de Prasae-route.

enhanced_description:
Thung Prong Thong is the Rayong stop that makes the province feel broader than coast and island marketing alone. The mangrove landscape gives the Klaeng and Prasae side a distinct visual identity, which is why this attraction works best as part of a dedicated wetland day rather than as something tacked onto a beach itinerary.

The site is most useful when you respect timing. Late light is part of why travelers come, and it turns the stop into something more atmospheric than a quick midday boardwalk pass.
detailed_history: TAT's current Rayong two-day route still keeps Thung Prong Thong in the official outline, which is a strong signal that the mangrove side remains one of the province's durable attractions.
visitor_experience: Expect a walkable nature stop with the best payoff coming from atmosphere and pacing rather than high-intensity activity. It is better as a slower final stop of a Prasae-side day than as a rushed box-checking exercise.

practical_info.how_to_get_there: Best reached by car or driver as part of a longer day on the Klaeng and Prasae side.
practical_info.nearby_attractions: Pairs naturally with Rayong Botanical Garden and other Prasae-side nature planning.
practical_info.food_options: Plan meals elsewhere on the route unless you have confirmed current local options in advance.
practical_info.shopping: This is a nature stop first, not a shopping stop.

insider_tips: Do this later in the day if you want the visual payoff people actually come for.
Keep it in the same route as the botanical garden rather than forcing it into a beach day.
Weather matters more here than at indoor city stops.
verified_note: Source-backed summary based on Tourism Authority of Thailand's current Rayong route framing. Reconfirm local access and conditions before travel.
cultural_significance: Thung Prong Thong helps keep Rayong's mangrove and estuary landscape visible in the province's travel story, which would otherwise become too beach-heavy.
